name: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
tagline: Open-weight 35B/3B multimodal MoE with thinking mode and a 262K-token context.
metaDescription: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is an Apache-2.0 open-weight MoE model (35B total, 3B active) with vision, a thinking mode, and a 262K-token context extensible to ~1M.
overview: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is an open-weight flagship model from Alibaba's Qwen team, released on 16 April 2026. It is a sparse Mixture-of-Experts model with 35 billion total parameters and only 3 billion activated per token, built from 40 layers with 256 experts (8 routed plus 1 shared activated per token) and a hybrid Gated DeltaNet / Gated Attention layout.

Unlike the earlier text-only Qwen3 line, this model is multimodal: it accepts text, images, and video through an integrated vision encoder and is classified on Hugging Face as image-text-to-text. It features a switchable thinking/reasoning mode that emits explicit thinking blocks before answering, which can be disabled, and a 'Thinking Preservation' mechanism that retains reasoning across turns to reduce overhead in iterative coding sessions.

The model supports a native context window of 262,144 tokens, extensible to roughly 1,010,000 tokens via YaRN scaling, with a recommended output budget of 32,768 tokens (up to 81,920 for hard math or competitive programming). All Qwen3.6 open-weight models, including this one, are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license on Hugging Face and ModelScope.
strengths: Apache-2.0 open weights, free for commercial use and self-hosting
Very efficient sparse MoE — only 3B of 35B parameters active per token
Native multimodal input (text, images, and video) via an integrated vision encoder
Switchable thinking mode plus Thinking Preservation for iterative agentic coding
Long context: 262K tokens natively, extensible to ~1M with YaRN
useCases: Agentic and repository-level coding with frontend workflow support
Multimodal document, image, and video understanding tasks
Self-hosted reasoning assistants where open Apache-2.0 weights are required
Long-context analysis up to the extended ~1M-token window

faq.q: Is Qwen3.6-35B-A3B open weight and free to use?
faq.a: Yes. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is released under the Apache 2.0 license, a permissive open-weight license that allows commercial use, modification, and redistribution. The weights are downloadable from Hugging Face and ModelScope, so you can run, fine-tune, and deploy the model yourself, including in commercial products and on-premise environments, without per-token fees to Alibaba.

It describes the model's Mixture-of-Experts design: 35 billion total parameters but only about 3 billion activated per token (A3B). At inference the router selects 8 of 256 experts plus 1 shared expert, so the model has the knowledge capacity of a large network while keeping the compute cost close to a 3B dense model, which makes it efficient to serve.

Yes. Unlike earlier text-only Qwen3 models, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is multimodal and accepts text, images, and video through an integrated vision encoder. On Hugging Face it is classified as an image-text-to-text model, and it can be served with vision support using inference frameworks such as SGLang and vLLM.

faq.q: How long is the context window?
faq.a: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B supports a native context window of 262,144 tokens, which can be extended to roughly 1,010,000 tokens using YaRN scaling. For output, the Qwen team recommends a budget of 32,768 tokens for standard queries and up to 81,920 tokens for complex mathematical or competitive programming problems.

versionHistory.note: Qwen3 series; dense + MoE, hybrid thinking modes
